text:
All eight games on the 2026-07-06 slate remain in progress at pull time, so today's board is still built entirely on the back-to-back near-HR pattern hitters put up on July 4th and July 5th. Bryson Stott leads the pack at a 68.5 expectancy score on a two-game streak: his 7/5 triple off the Royals left the bat at 106.2 mph and traveled 406 feet, a shot Statcast marks as a would-be homer in 19 of 30 parks, and his max-EV slope (+5.0) is still climbing. Right behind him, Phillies teammate Brandon Marsh (66.0) and Cardinals shortstop Masyn Winn (66.0) both barreled balls in the 396-406 foot range with exit velocities north of 104 mph on 7/5, each also riding a two-game near-HR streak with a would-be-homer park count of 6-7.

Carlos Cortes (59.3) is the name to watch for pure contact quality rather than raw distance: he has posted a near-HR extra-base hit in back-to-back games (a 365-foot double at 105.7 mph on 7/5, a 352-foot double at 93.8 mph on 7/4), giving him the highest intensity reading of the group's XBH-heavy profiles even though his would-be-homer counts are modest. Cody Bellinger, Adley Rutschman, Jose Altuve and Miguel Vargas round out the 56.0 tier on the strength of one strong near-HR game each in the last two days, though their near-HR slopes are flat to negative, suggesting the model expects some regression there rather than a building trend.

One tier down, a cluster of one-day flaggers is trending sharply upward on the slope metrics that matter most: Royce Lewis (403 ft, 109.4 mph, near-HR slope +1.25), Riley Greene (431 ft, 111.2 mph, two homers in the trailing 7 days already), Bo Bichette and Gabriel Rincones Jr. all show positive near-HR and would-be-park slopes even though their streak resets to one game — these are the names most likely to jump into the top tier if they repeat today. Sam Antonacci is a wildcard with the single largest max-EV slope in the field (+31.9) after his best batted-ball outing of the window.

No flagged players from the prior pull converted to an actual home run in this window, so there are no conversions to report and the model's rolling hit-rate track record remains statistically thin (recent_hits empty, insufficient graded samples for a rate yet). With today's eight games still unfinished at run time, the real test of this board comes once 2026-07-06's own final data lands and these carryover streaks either extend or break.
